questions.stem:
—Why don't you watch the movie with us?  
—Because I ______ it twice, and I ______ it with my parents last week.
questions.explanation: 第一空：twice（两次）是现在完成时的标志词，表示过去动作对现在的影响（已经看过了，所以不想再看），用have seen；第二空：last week（上周）是一般过去时的标志词，用watched。选项B和D第一空错误，选项C第二空错误。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The factory ______ we visited last week produces environmentally friendly bags.
questions.explanation: 先行词factory（工厂）是物，不是人，排除A(who)和B(whom)；D(whose)表示'...的'，在从句中作定语，而本句从句缺少visited的宾语，应用which/that引导。which在定语从句中作宾语，指代factory。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: These old books ______ to the children in poor areas next month.
questions.explanation: 主语books（书）与send（送）是被动关系，应用被动语态；next month（下个月）是将来时标志，所以用一般将来时的被动语态will be sent。A是主动语态，C是现在进行时主动，D是一般过去时被动，时间不符。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem:
—Is that Mr. Wang knocking at the door?  
—It ______ be him. He has gone to Beijing on business.
questions.explanation: 根据'He has gone to Beijing'（他去北京出差了，还没回来）可知，敲门的不可能是他。can't表示'不可能'，用于否定推测；must表示'一定'（肯定推测），与句意相反；might表示'可能'，语气不确定；mustn't表示'禁止'，不用于推测。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: My mother is very busy with her work, so she has to ______ her exercise time to early mornings before going to the office.
questions.explanation: 根据上下文'很忙'和'早上上班前'可知，她需要'挤'出时间锻炼。squeeze意为'挤出（时间）'；spread意为'展开，传播'；waste意为'浪费'；expand意为'扩展'。根据语境逻辑，选B。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: If you add the prefix 'un-' to the word 'healthy', you get a word meaning ______.
questions.explanation: 前缀un-表示'不，无，相反'，unhealthy意为'不健康的'，即not healthy。A是更健康，C是充满健康，D是拥有健康，均不符合。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: We all ______ Tom because he won the first prize in the English speech competition.
questions.explanation: look up to意为'尊敬，钦佩'；look after意为'照顾'；look for意为'寻找'；look down upon意为'看不起'。根据'赢得一等奖'可知，我们都'钦佩'Tom，选A。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.explanation: 根据上下文，学校开展活动是为了'保护'环境，protect the environment是固定搭配。produce生产，pollute污染，provide提供，均不符合语境。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started an interesting project called 'Green Campus Action'. The goal was to teach students how to (8)___ the environment through small daily changes. At first, many students didn't take it seriously. They thought one person's actions couldn't make any difference.

However, things changed after a special class meeting. Our teacher showed us some shocking photos of plastic waste in the ocean. She explained that every piece of plastic we throw away might (9)___ up in the sea and harm sea animals. 'If each of us uses one less plastic bag a week,' she said, 'we can (10)___ hundreds of bags from entering the ocean every year.'

From that day on, my classmates and I began to bring our own cloth bags to school. We also started a recycling corner in our classroom. To our surprise, other classes (11)___ our idea and soon the whole school joined in. Now, our school produces 50% less plastic waste than before.

This experience taught me that environmental protection starts with small steps. We don't need to do big things to change the world. (12)___, we just need to change our daily habits. Small actions, when multiplied by millions of people, can transform the world.

questions.explanation: end up是固定短语，意为'最终成为/到达'，这里指塑料最终进入大海。put up张贴，take up占据/开始从事，give up放弃，均不符合语境。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started an interesting project called 'Green Campus Action'. The goal was to teach students how to (8)___ the environment through small daily changes. At first, many students didn't take it seriously. They thought one person's actions couldn't make any difference.

However, things changed after a special class meeting. Our teacher showed us some shocking photos of plastic waste in the ocean. She explained that every piece of plastic we throw away might (9)___ up in the sea and harm sea animals. 'If each of us uses one less plastic bag a week,' she said, 'we can (10)___ hundreds of bags from entering the ocean every year.'

From that day on, my classmates and I began to bring our own cloth bags to school. We also started a recycling corner in our classroom. To our surprise, other classes (11)___ our idea and soon the whole school joined in. Now, our school produces 50% less plastic waste than before.

This experience taught me that environmental protection starts with small steps. We don't need to do big things to change the world. (12)___, we just need to change our daily habits. Small actions, when multiplied by millions of people, can transform the world.

questions.explanation: prevent...from...是固定搭配，意为'阻止...做某事'。save...from...拯救...免于...，stop...from...阻止（stop可用prevent替换，但此处结构为prevent bags from entering），keep...from...阻止（但keep更强调使保持某种状态）。根据语境'阻止塑料袋进入海洋'，prevent最合适。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started an interesting project called 'Green Campus Action'. The goal was to teach students how to (8)___ the environment through small daily changes. At first, many students didn't take it seriously. They thought one person's actions couldn't make any difference.

However, things changed after a special class meeting. Our teacher showed us some shocking photos of plastic waste in the ocean. She explained that every piece of plastic we throw away might (9)___ up in the sea and harm sea animals. 'If each of us uses one less plastic bag a week,' she said, 'we can (10)___ hundreds of bags from entering the ocean every year.'

From that day on, my classmates and I began to bring our own cloth bags to school. We also started a recycling corner in our classroom. To our surprise, other classes (11)___ our idea and soon the whole school joined in. Now, our school produces 50% less plastic waste than before.

This experience taught me that environmental protection starts with small steps. We don't need to do big things to change the world. (12)___, we just need to change our daily habits. Small actions, when multiplied by millions of people, can transform the world.

questions.explanation: 根据后文'soon the whole school joined in'（全校都加入）可知，其他班级'效仿/复制'了我们的想法。copied意为'复制，效仿'；refused拒绝，doubted怀疑，forgot忘记，均与后文逻辑不符。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started an interesting project called 'Green Campus Action'. The goal was to teach students how to (8)___ the environment through small daily changes. At first, many students didn't take it seriously. They thought one person's actions couldn't make any difference.

However, things changed after a special class meeting. Our teacher showed us some shocking photos of plastic waste in the ocean. She explained that every piece of plastic we throw away might (9)___ up in the sea and harm sea animals. 'If each of us uses one less plastic bag a week,' she said, 'we can (10)___ hundreds of bags from entering the ocean every year.'

From that day on, my classmates and I began to bring our own cloth bags to school. We also started a recycling corner in our classroom. To our surprise, other classes (11)___ our idea and soon the whole school joined in. Now, our school produces 50% less plastic waste than before.

This experience taught me that environmental protection starts with small steps. We don't need to do big things to change the world. (12)___, we just need to change our daily habits. Small actions, when multiplied by millions of people, can transform the world.

questions.explanation: 前文说'不需要做大事'，后文说'只需要改变日常习惯'，前后是转折替代关系，instead意为'相反，反而，取而代之'。however然而（表转折，通常后有逗号且语义侧重不同），therefore因此（表因果），otherwise否则（表条件），均不如instead贴切。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started an interesting project called 'Green Campus Action'. The goal was to teach students how to (8)___ the environment through small daily changes. At first, many students didn't take it seriously. They thought one person's actions couldn't make any difference.

However, things changed after a special class meeting. Our teacher showed us some shocking photos of plastic waste in the ocean. She explained that every piece of plastic we throw away might (9)___ up in the sea and harm sea animals. 'If each of us uses one less plastic bag a week,' she said, 'we can (10)___ hundreds of bags from entering the ocean every year.'

From that day on, my classmates and I began to bring our own cloth bags to school. We also started a recycling corner in our classroom. To our surprise, other classes (11)___ our idea and soon the whole school joined in. Now, our school produces 50% less plastic waste than before.

questions.stem: Why did Bosco decide to join the Science Club?
options: A. Because his parents forced him to join.
B. Because he wanted to make more friends.
C. Because he found science experiments interesting after the open day.
D. Because he needed extra credits for his final grade.
questions.explanation: 根据文章第二段'After the open day, I realized that science experiments were not boring at all. In fact, they were full of surprises... I decided to join the Science Club immediately.'可知，Bosco是在开放日之后发现科学实验很有趣，才决定加入的。A（父母强迫）、B（想交更多朋友）、D（需要学分）文中均未提及。
questions.label: 阅读-细节理解
questions.group_passage:
My name is Bosco, and I'm an eighth-grade student at Sunshine Middle School. Like many of my classmates, I used to think science was boring and difficult. All those formulas and theories seemed to have nothing to do with my daily life.

Everything changed last semester when our school held a Science Open Day. Different clubs set up booths (摊位) to show their projects. The Science Club demonstrated how to make a simple battery using lemons. After the open day, I realized that science experiments were not boring at all. In fact, they were full of surprises and could help solve real-life problems. I decided to join the Science Club immediately.

In the club, we meet every Wednesday afternoon. Last month, we worked on a project about water purification (净化). We built a simple filter (过滤器) using sand, stones, and cotton cloth. It was amazing to see dirty water become clear after passing through our homemade filter. Our teacher, Mr. Li, told us that many villages in remote areas still lack clean drinking water. 'Science is not just about passing exams,' he said. 'It's about making the world better.'

This experience has changed my attitude towards learning. I no longer memorize facts just for tests. Instead, I try to understand how knowledge can be applied in real situations. Next month, our club plans to visit a local science museum. I'm really looking forward to learning more about renewable energy and environmental protection.

questions.stem: What can we infer about Bosco's attitude towards science before joining the club?
options: A. He was very interested in scientific theories.
B. He considered it unrelated to practical life.
C. He was afraid of doing experiments in the lab.
D. He thought science was easier than other subjects.
questions.explanation: 根据第一段'All those formulas and theories seemed to have nothing to do with my daily life'（所有那些公式和理论似乎与我的日常生活无关）可推断，Bosco之前认为科学与实际生活无关。A（对理论很感兴趣）与原文相反；C（害怕做实验）文中未提及；D（认为比其他科目容易）文中说' difficult'，与D相反。
questions.label: 阅读-推理判断
questions.group_passage:

questions.stem: What does the underlined word 'demonstrated' in Paragraph 2 probably mean?
options: A. Taught a lesson about something.
B. Showed how to do something by doing it.
C. Explained by writing on the blackboard.
questions.explanation: 根据上下文'The Science Club demonstrated how to make a simple battery using lemons'（科学社演示了如何用柠檬制作简易电池），demonstrated意为'演示，展示'，即通过实际操作展示如何做某事。showed how to do something by doing it（通过做来展示如何做）符合此意。A（教课）、C（黑板写字解释）、D（与小组成员讨论）均不准确。
questions.label: 阅读-词义猜测
questions.group_passage:

questions.stem: What is the best title for this passage?
options: A. The History of Our School Clubs
B. How I Changed My View on Science
C. The Importance of Clean Drinking Water
D. A Visit to the Local Science Museum
questions.explanation: 文章主要讲述了Bosco从认为科学枯燥难懂，到通过开放日和俱乐部活动改变看法，认识到科学可以解决实际问题的心路历程。A（学校社团历史）范围太大；C（清洁饮用水的重要性）只是文中一个例子；D（参观科学博物馆）是计划发生的事，不是主要内容。B（我如何改变对科学的看法）最能概括全文主旨。
questions.label: 阅读-主旨大意
questions.group_passage:
My name is Bosco, and I'm an eighth-grade student at Sunshine Middle School. Like many of my classmates, I used to think science was boring and difficult. All those formulas and theories seemed to have nothing to do with my daily life.

Everything changed last semester when our school held a Science Open Day. Different clubs set up booths (摊位) to show their projects. The Science Club demonstrated how to make a simple battery using lemons. After the open day, I realized that science experiments were not boring at all. In fact, they were full of surprises and could help solve real-life problems. I decided to join the Science Club immediately.

In the club, we meet every Wednesday afternoon. Last month, we worked on a project about water purification (净化). We built a simple filter (过滤器) using sand, stones, and cotton cloth. It was amazing to see dirty water become clear after passing through our homemade filter. Our teacher, Mr. Li, told us that many villages in remote areas still lack clean drinking water. 'Science is not just about passing exams,' he said. 'It's about making the world better.'

This experience has changed my attitude towards learning. I no longer memorize facts just for tests. Instead, I try to understand how knowledge can be applied in real situations. Next month, our club plans to visit a local science museum. I'm really looking forward to learning more about renewable energy and environmental protection.

options: A. However, it is not always easy to find the right balance.
B. As a result, they often feel stressed and tired.
C. In addition, taking short breaks can help improve concentration.
D. First, you should make a list of your daily tasks and set priorities.
E. On the other hand, some students spend too much time on hobbies.
questions.explanation: 本段主题是'如何平衡学习与爱好'，接下来应该是具体建议。根据后文的'Second'（第二）可知，此处应该是'First'（第一），构成顺序关系。D选项'First, you should make a list...'符合段落衔接和逻辑顺序。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
As middle school students, we all have our favorite hobbies, such as playing basketball, reading, or painting. However, we also have a lot of homework and exams to prepare for. How can we balance study time and hobby time? Here is some useful advice.

(17)___ Write down what you need to do for school and how long each task takes. Then, schedule specific time slots for your hobbies. This way, you won't feel guilty when you're having fun, and you won't forget to study.

(18)___ Many students try to study for hours without stopping, but this is actually not efficient. Our brains need rest to work well. When you feel tired after studying math or English, spend 20 minutes on your hobby to refresh your mind.

(19)___ Some students focus only on their studies and become bookworms. Others spend all their free time playing games and ignore their schoolwork. Both extremes are harmful.

(20)___ Try to find a healthy middle ground. Remember, hobbies can make you more creative and relaxed, which will actually help you study better in the long run.

options: A. However, it is not always easy to find the right balance.
B. As a result, they often feel stressed and tired.
C. In addition, taking short breaks can help improve concentration.
D. First, you should make a list of your daily tasks and set priorities.
E. On the other hand, some students spend too much time on hobbies.
questions.explanation: 本段后文提到'学习数小时后不停下来'、'大脑需要休息'、'花20分钟在爱好上 refresh your mind'，可知本段主题是休息的重要性。C选项'In addition, taking short breaks can help improve concentration'（此外，短暂休息有助于提高专注力）符合本段主旨，且In addition表示递进，承接上文的第一个建议。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:

(18)___ Many students try to study for hours without stopping, but this is actually not efficient. Our brains need rest to work well. When you feel tired after studying math or English, spend 20 minutes on your hobby to refresh your mind.

(19)___ Some students focus only on their studies and become bookworms. Others spend all their free time playing games and ignore their schoolwork. Both extremes are harmful.

(20)___ Try to find a healthy middle ground. Remember, hobbies can make you more creative and relaxed, which will actually help you study better in the long run.

options: A. However, it is not always easy to find the right balance.
B. As a result, they often feel stressed and tired.
C. In addition, taking short breaks can help improve concentration.
D. First, you should make a list of your daily tasks and set priorities.
E. On the other hand, some students spend too much time on hobbies.
questions.explanation: 本段后文提到'有些学生只关注学习变成书呆子，其他人则花所有时间玩游戏忽视学业'，这是在说两种极端情况，即找到平衡并不容易。A选项'However, it is not always easy to find the right balance'（然而，找到正确的平衡并不总是容易的）作为本段主题句，引出下文的具体例子，符合逻辑。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:

(17)___ Write down what you need to do for school and how long each task takes. Then, schedule specific time slots for your hobbies. This way, you won't feel guilty when you're having fun, and you won't forget to study.

(18)___ Many students try to study for hours without stopping, but this is actually not efficient. Our brains need rest to work well. When you feel tired after studying math or English, spend 20 minutes on your hobby to refresh your mind.

options: A. However, it is not always easy to find the right balance.
B. As a result, they often feel stressed and tired.
C. In addition, taking short breaks can help improve concentration.
D. First, you should make a list of your daily tasks and set priorities.
E. On the other hand, some students spend too much time on hobbies.
questions.explanation: 本段前文提到'Both extremes are harmful'（两种极端都有害），此处应该是结果。B选项'As a result, they often feel stressed and tired'（结果，他们经常感到压力和疲惫）是前文所述极端行为的后果，且与后文'Try to find a healthy middle ground'（尝试找到健康的中间地带）形成因果逻辑：因为会感到压力疲惫，所以要找到平衡点。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
